The prophet calls for national repentance as the only way to prevent national ruin. A nation that does not desire God, that has no desires toward God, is not desirous of his favor and grace, has no mind to repent and reform. Or, it is not desirable because it has nothing to recommend itself to God; to whom God might justly say, 'Depart from me,' but he says, 'Gather together to me so you may seek my face.' We know what God's decree will bring against impenitent sinners, therefore it greatly concerns all to repent in the accepted time. How careful we should all be to seek peace with God before the Holy Spirit withdraws from us or ceases to strive with us; before the day of grace is over, or the day of life; before our everlasting state is determined! Let the poor, despised, and afflicted seek the Lord, and seek to understand and keep his commandments better, that they may be more humbled for their sins. The chief hope of deliverance from national judgments rests upon prayer.

Those are truly in a woeful condition who have the word of the Lord against them, because no word of his will fall to the ground. God will restore his people to their rights, even though they have been kept from them for a long time. It has been the common experience of God’s people in all ages to be reproached and reviled. God shall be worshipped not only by all Israel and the strangers who join them, but also by the heathen. Remote nations must be held accountable for the wrongs done to God's people. The sufferings of the insolent and haughty in prosperity are unpitied and unlamented. But all the desolations of flourishing nations will make way for the overturning of Satan’s kingdom. Let us improve our advantages, and expect the performance of every promise, praying that our Father’s name may be hallowed everywhere, over all the earth.
